name: Argument Strength
description: Evaluates the quality, persuasiveness, and completeness of patent prosecution arguments in Office Action responses.

dimensions.name: legal_reasoning
dimensions.description: Quality of the legal reasoning and logical structure of arguments

criteria.1: No coherent legal reasoning; arguments are conclusory without supporting analysis
criteria.2: Basic legal reasoning present but contains logical gaps or non-sequiturs; arguments would not persuade an examiner
criteria.3: Adequate legal reasoning with a clear logical flow; addresses the main issues but may miss secondary arguments
criteria.4: Strong legal reasoning with well-structured arguments; addresses primary and secondary issues; would likely persuade a reasonable examiner
criteria.5: Exceptional legal reasoning with airtight logic; anticipates and addresses counterarguments; uses multiple independent lines of argument; would persuade even a skeptical examiner

dimensions.name: factual_grounding
dimensions.description: How well arguments are grounded in the specific facts of the case (claim language, prior art disclosures, specification)

criteria.1: Generic arguments not tied to the specific claims or prior art; could apply to any rejection
criteria.2: References specific claims or prior art but analysis is superficial; does not engage with the details
criteria.3: Engages with specific claim limitations and prior art disclosures; identifies key differences but analysis could be deeper
criteria.4: Thoroughly analyzes specific claim limitations against prior art disclosures; pinpoints exact missing elements or teachings
criteria.5: Expert-level factual analysis; precisely maps each claim limitation to specific prior art paragraphs/columns; identifies subtle but critical distinctions

dimensions.name: completeness
dimensions.description: Does the response address all rejected claims, all grounds of rejection, and all required elements?

criteria.1: Addresses fewer than half of the rejections or claims; major omissions
criteria.2: Addresses most rejections but misses at least one; some claims left unaddressed
criteria.3: Addresses all rejections and claims at a basic level; may lack depth on some points
criteria.4: Comprehensively addresses all rejections and claims with adequate depth
criteria.5: Exhaustively addresses every rejection, every claim, and includes proactive arguments (e.g., unexpected results, secondary considerations, teaching away)

dimensions.name: persuasiveness
dimensions.description: Overall persuasive impact of the response as evaluated by a patent examiner or PTAB judge

criteria.1: Not persuasive; an examiner would maintain the rejection without further consideration
criteria.2: Slightly persuasive; raises a point worth considering but ultimately unconvincing
criteria.3: Moderately persuasive; an examiner would need to thoughtfully address the arguments in the next action
criteria.4: Highly persuasive; a reasonable examiner would likely withdraw the rejection or invite an interview
criteria.5: Compelling; arguments are so strong that maintaining the rejection would be difficult to sustain on appeal

dimensions.name: professional_quality
dimensions.description: Writing quality, tone, organization, and adherence to patent prosecution conventions

criteria.1: Unprofessional tone or format; would embarrass the practitioner if filed
criteria.2: Generally professional but with notable formatting or tone issues
criteria.3: Adequate professional quality; follows basic patent prosecution conventions
criteria.4: High professional quality; well-organized with clear headings and respectful tone
criteria.5: Exemplary professional quality; reads like a response from a top-tier patent firm with perfect organization, clear structure, and authoritative tone
